destinations.evidence: The port now defines AUDIO_RESPONSE_DURATION_OFFSET as AudioResponse slot 6 and decodes the u32 outputDurationMs field into pricing seconds, returning 0 for 0 ms and rounding any nonzero partial second up.
destinations.snippet: pub fn extract_audio_duration_from_delivery(delivery_result: Option<&Bytes>) -> Option<u64> { ... Some(if duration_ms == 0 { 0 } else { (u64::from(duration_ms) + 999) / 1000 }) }

destinations.evidence: Focused unit tests cover 0 ms, sub-second, exact-second, over-second, and missing/too-short delivery results.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port for the narrow duration-extraction invariant: audio delivery results now expose a shared extractor that reads slot 6 as milliseconds and returns rounded-up seconds for pricing. The broader audio Phase 2 builder/verifier fee-path work remains tracked by later audio invariants.

related_but_insufficient_code: AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE is registered and delivery config decoding treats audio like other two-phase precompiles, but calculate_async_fees_phase2 still does not route audio through a dedicated duration-priced fee calculator.
The broader payload-builder and block-verifier audio Phase 2 fee paths remain tracked by subsequent audio invariants.

notes: Mapped after local parity fix for the shared extractor. The port still lacks the larger audio escrow and Phase 2 builder/verifier pricing paths, which remain represented by subsequent audio invariants.
rationale: The duration extractor changed from returning the raw u32 value to treating it as milliseconds and converting to rounded-up seconds. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The response field layout is codec-specific, but duration units directly affect executor fees.
statement: Audio Phase 2 pricing must interpret delivery duration as milliseconds from the response and round nonzero duration up to whole seconds for fee calculation.
suggested_falsification_tests: Decode audio delivery results with durations 0 ms, 1 ms, 1000 ms, and 1001 ms and assert extracted pricing durations are 0, 1, 1, and 2 seconds respectively.
summary: Audio duration extraction for rounded-second pricing is mapped in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Local ritual-async-fees unit tests encode slot-6 outputDurationMs values 0, 1, 999, 1000, 1001, and 61000 and assert the extractor returns 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, and 61 seconds; missing and too-short delivery results return None.
title: Audio duration priced in rounded seconds
